title: Ansible-Based Splunk Deployment Automation
summary: Automated Splunk forwarder deployment across 300+ servers using Ansible, replacing manual configuration.

content.value: Zycus operated 300+ Linux and Windows servers that each required a Splunk Universal Forwarder for centralized log collection. Manual deployment took days per release cycle and was inconsistent across environments.

Wrote Ansible playbooks to automate forwarder installation, version upgrades, and input configuration across all server types. An inventory was maintained in a dynamic Ansible inventory script that pulled server lists from the CMDB.

Playbooks handled OS-specific nuances (RPM on RHEL, MSI on Windows), certificate rotation for TLS-encrypted forwarding, and idempotent configuration management so re-runs caused no disruption. Deployed via Jenkins pipeline with per-environment approval gates.
Deployment time across all 300+ servers reduced from 3 days to under 4 hours. Configuration consistency improved to 100%, resolving chronic log-gap incidents that had plagued the security team.
